stem: A energy power grid uses 3 parallel generators for redundancy. The power maintained requires at least one generator to function. generator 1 has a reliability of 78%. generator 2 has a reliability between 78% and 87% (uncertain due to limited operational data). generator 3 has a reliability of 77%. What is the overall probability that the power maintained?

ground_truth.answer: P(power maintained) ranges from 0.9889 to 0.9934. Point estimate: 0.9909.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 generators fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.22 × 0.22 × 0.23 = 0.0111. P(works) = 0.9889.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.22 × 0.13 × 0.23 = 0.0066. P(works) = 0.9934.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
